articles.title: A United Front Against “Settled Expectations”
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇

本周，七份法庭之友意见书被提交，以支持谷歌的请愿书，该请愿书挑战“既定预期”学说——该学说于2025年从迷雾中出现，成为IPR请愿书死亡的主要原因。请愿书提出两个问题。

PTO于5月22日放弃回应权利。VirtaMove的反对方意见书及支持被申请人的法庭之友意见书尚未到期，最可能的下一步进展是法院要求提交答辩。

尽管每份意见书角度略有不同，但法庭之友意见书都围绕两个相同论点：（1）六年规则是机构无权制定的法定限制期限之外的时效规定；（2）联邦巡回法院将《美国法典》第35编第314(d)条作为禁止任何法院审查越权行为的障碍是错误的。

案件的基本困难在于，最高法院和联邦巡回法院迄今为止都对USPTO关于立案的决定高度尊重。国会隐含地赋予了局长这种尊重，并使其可上诉。然而，这些决定是由局长而非法官做出的。这意味着法院需要在判决中对抗另外两个政府分支。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

This past week, seven amicus briefs were filed in support of Google's petition challenging the "Settled Expectations" doctrine that emerged from the mist in 2025 as the leading cause of death of IPR petitions. The petition asks two questions:

  • whether the USPTO lacks statutory authority to deny IPR institution based on a patent owner's "settled expectations" arising from the age of the patent, and
  • whether courts may review a denial that rests on a ground contrary to statute.

The PTO waived its right to respond on May 22. VirtaMove's brief in opposition and any amici supporting the respondent are not yet due, and the most probable next development is a call for a response from the Court.

Although each has a somewhat different perspective, the amici converge on the same two propositions (1) the six-year rule is an extra-statutory limitations period the agency had no authority to create, and (2) that the Federal Circuit was wrong to treat 35 U.S.C. § 314(d) as a bar to any court ever addressing the ultra vires action.

The basic difficulty in the the case is that both the Supreme Court and Federal Circuit have thus far been highly deferential to USPTO decisions on institution.   Congress implicitly provided the Director with deference and made that appealable. And, these decisions are being made by the Director, not judges.  That means the court would need to be pushing back against the two other of government in its decision.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
